title: digitalis minor
intro: Digitalis minor, also known as the lesser foxglove, is a biennial plant in the plantain family that is native to Europe and western Asia. It has medicinal properties due to its content of glycosides, which are similar to those found in the more commonly known foxglove (Digitalis purpurea). The health benefits of Digitalis minor include its use in treating heart conditions, such as atrial fibrillation and congestive heart failure, and its potential to lower blood pressure. Digitalis minor can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or dried extract, and its use should be guided by a healthcare professional due to its potential for toxicity and interactions with other medications.
